summary: Add debugger message to allow re-running a project and reconnecting to debugger

description.text:
This contains just the debugger part that was originally in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/107671

This aims to allow a project launched from the editor, to re-launch itself with different arguments, while reconnecting to the debugger and ensuring that the embedded game view still works after being re-launched

The ultimate goal is to allow the [godot_openxr_vendor](https://github.com/GodotVR/godot_openxr_vendors) extension to simulate running a hybrid immersive/flat VR app on desktop, so you don't need to deploy to the headset for testing.

See https://github.com/GodotVR/godot_openxr_vendors/pull/312 for the rest of the implementation of that (also depends on https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/107671)

summary: ui_select/ui_accept not properly marked handled on usage by multiple default UI Nodes

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible on 4.4.1 and 4.5 Beta 3

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4080 SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7602) - AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D 8-Core Processor (16 threads)

### Issue description

**CheckButton, CheckBox, and OptionButton** nodes respond to ui_select/ui_accept, but do not mark the event as handled, resulting in the input leaking through to normal gameplay nodes' _unhandled_input functions.  Other nodes may also have this issue but I don't know the entire collection of UI nodes well enough to perform an exhaustive test.

Curiously, OptionButton leaks the event only when opening the OptionButton menu, but does not leak the event/properly marks it as handled when pressed within the then-activated OptionButton menu.

### Steps to reproduce

Run MRP:
1. (Bug)
    - Click or otherwise select one of the CheckButton/CheckBox UI elements.
    - Press Space Bar
    - Note that the button activated, but the on-screen character/Godot Icon also had its _unhandled_input triggered, resulting in it rotating clockwise.
2. (Bug)
    - Press the Enter Key
    - Observe the same behavior with events double-activated.

3. (Half-Bugged)
    - Select the OptionButton
    - Press Enter or Space Bar
    - Note the event leaking over to the Icon (causing the Icon to rotate)
    - Navigate within the OptionButton sub-menu with the arrow keys.
    - Select an option within the OptionButton sub-menu with Enter or Space.
    - Note that the input event was correctly consumed while in the sub-menu/did not leak through to the Icon node.
3. (Correct Behavior Sample)
    - Select the LineEdit Node.
    - Press Enter.  Observe that the the UI responds, and the input is marked as consumed (Godot Icon does not rotate)
    - Exit the LineEdit node.
4. (Correct Behavior Sample)
    - Press the Arrow Keys
    - Note that the input actions only flow through to the Icon node when reaching the end of the UI row/when the UI does not make use of them.

Or for manual repro steps:
- Create a [OptionButton/CheckButton/CheckBox] node
- Create an input binding for the Space Bar key.
- Create another node that overrides _unhandled_input
- In that _unhandled_input, check for the newly created input action.
- Run the project.  Click on the UI node.  Press Space
- Note that the InputAction in question is still registered as both active and not cancelled in the _unhandled_event function

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[ui-input-bugs-mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21353337/ui-input-bugs-mrp.zip)

summary: `RenderingServer.multimesh_instance_get_transform()` uses CPU-cached value rather than GPU buffer data

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducable in 4.4.1. and v4.5.beta3.official


### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Linux Mint 21.3 (Virginia) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce 930MX (nvidia; 570.169) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-7820HQ CPU @ 2.90GHz (8 threads)

### Issue description

A multimesh is created in RenderingServer and the positions of the multimesh instances are updated in a compute shader. This part is working fine, I can update mesh locations in the compute shader and they are being drawn correctly.

 I  try getting the location of individual meshes so e.g. the camera can follow one planet as a test:

```gdscript
var EarthPosition=RenderingServer.multimesh_instance_get_transform(planet_Multimesh,4)
look_at(EarthPosition.origin)
```

In the MVP a position is just retrieved and printed when the space bar is pressed.

The camera is stuck looking at the starting position of that planet, which of course has moved away due to the compute shader. It looks like multimesh_instance_get_transform is retrieving a CPU copy of the buffer.

While a more complete indirect multimesh might allow draw calls to use a SSBO directly, I have used the multimesh buffer as this appears to be the only way to do multiple draw calls using GPU created data.


MrCDK confirmed on forum that:
 [servers/rendering/renderer_rd/storage_rd/mesh_storage.cpp](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/71a9948157dc2d5cc71fcb456c9d96656678757d/servers/rendering/renderer_rd/storage_rd/mesh_storage.cpp#L1995-L2003)  does not update if cached buffer is still present. But there does not seem to be any use case for instance_get_transform() if the instance positions have not been changed outside the GPU. I suspect the same is true for get_color and get_custom.

[Forum post with link to Godot source](https://forum.godotengine.org/t/multimesh-instance-get-transform-not-updating-when-buffer-updated-in-compute-shader/116825/1)

A quick fix would be to change MeshStorage::_multimesh_make_local

and remove :
```cpp
	if (multimesh->data_cache.size() > 0) {
		return; //already local
	}
```
But it would possibly be better to retrieve the individual instance data when needed to reduce transfers.

### Steps to reproduce

Create Multimesh

Get Multimesh buffer RID and use it as a storage buffer for a compute shader to update mesh positions on GPU.

Retrieve a mesh instance transform using RenderingServer.multimesh_instance_get_transform



### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[MVPMMBug.tar.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21353227/MVPMMBug.tar.gz)

summary: Profiler panning/zooming doesn't work with a trackpad

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Repro: 4.5b2

### System information

macOS 15.0, macbook pro

### Issue description

The profiler graph can't be zoomed/panned with a macbook trackpad. I can click around in it to select frames, but..
- click+drag does nothing
- scrolling up/down does nothing
- pinching in/out does nothing
- right click+drag does nothing

Essentially, there doesn't seem to be any way of actually navigating that graph without a mouse. I've plugged one in to test and confirmed that it works fine with a mouse.

### Steps to reproduce

Run the profiler on any project with a macbook.

Try and fail to zoom/pan in the profiler graph.

Be sad.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

n/a

summary: Game crashes when right clicking after shrinking game window with Window methods

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in v4.4.1.stable.mono.official [49a5bc7b6], v4.4.2.rc.custom_build [e531f3eb7], v4.5.beta3.official [4d1f26e1f]

Custom build was tested without any modifications from source

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta3 - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7283) - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-13900H (20 threads) - 63.67 GiB memory

### Issue description

When shrinking a game window with `Window.start_resize()` from a left mouse click and shrinking the window from the bottom or right edge (corners included) to a size smaller than the position initially clicked, and then right clicking in the window without refocusing crashes the game without printing a message to the editor. The game window must not be embedded to cause this behavior.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7e3ad2a2-a608-4af6-9e77-19207c981cb3

The same crash can be caused with `Window.start_drag()` from a left mouse click if the drag operation undocks a window causing it to shrink to a size smaller than the position initially clicked, and then right clicking within the window.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1c36089b-d7b0-499c-b505-c5bf2fb57ed7

Expected Behavior:
The right click operation goes through in the position clicked within the window, and the game does not crash

Additional Information:
- Could not reproduce on MacOS in 4.4.1 stable
- Refocusing the window after resizing (e.g. alt-tabbing) prevents the crash until another shrink occurs
- Resizing the window using the usual window edges (not via a `Window` method) does not cause this crash
- The crash does not occur if there are no `Control` nodes in the scene or all `Control` nodes have their `mouse_filter` set to `Ignore`
- The crash comes from `viewport.cpp` within `void Viewport::_gui_input_event(Ref<InputEvent> p_event)` where `gui.mouse_focus` is `nullptr`. The assignment of `gui.mouse_focus` to `nullptr` seems to happen from `Control *Viewport::gui_find_control(const Point2 &p_global)` when left click is released after shrinking the window via `Window.start_resize` and `Window.start_drag` (after a shrink from undocking), but not when the window is expanded or not resized from those same methods

### Steps to reproduce

Crash with `Window.start_resize()`:
1. In a new Godot project, create a User Interface scene and attach a script to it
2. Override _input()
3. In _input(), call `get_window().start_resize(DisplayServer.WINDOW_EDGE_BOTTOM_RIGHT)` on Left mouse down
4. Start the game without embedding
5. Grow and then shrink the window by clicking within the game bounds with left click (triggering `start_resize()`)
6. Right click within the game bounds to cause the crash
```gdscript
extends Control

func _input(event: InputEvent) -> void:
	if (event is InputEventMouse):
		var mouse_event = event as InputEventMouse;
		if(mouse_event.button_mask == MOUSE_BUTTON_LEFT):
			get_window().start_resize(DisplayServer.WINDOW_EDGE_BOTTOM_RIGHT);
```

Crash with `Window.start_drag()`:
1. In a new Godot project, create a User Interface scene and attach a script to it
2. Override _input()
3. In _input(), call `get_window().start_drag()` on Left mouse down
4. Start the game without embedding
5. Dock the window to full screen by dragging it to the top by clicking within the game bounds with left click (triggering `start_drag()`)
6. Undock the window by clicking within the game bounds with left click (triggering `start_drag()`) and dragging down
7. Right click within the game bounds to cause the crash
```gdscript
extends Control

func _input(event: InputEvent) -> void:
	if (event is InputEventMouse):
		var mouse_event = event as InputEventMouse;
		if(mouse_event.button_mask == MOUSE_BUTTON_LEFT):
			get_window().start_drag();
```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[resize-crash.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21350639/resize-crash.zip)

summary: [Web] Make `emscriptenThreadPool` and `godotThreadPool` values automatic

description.text:
> [!WARNING]
>Needs testing. I had issues testing the fix on iOS and iPadOS, but I don't think it's related.

Instead of exposing `emscriptenThreadPool` and `godotThreadPool` in export settings, this PR makes it so that it uses `navigator.hardwareConcurrency` as a base, with minimum values.

Fixes #108484.

> [!NOTE]
> The PR also makes the `Clear persistent data` button actually clear the service worker. Needed to test the fix on mobile platforms.

summary: Some Shortcut Bindings Cannot be Triggered

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.4.1 & 4.5 beta 2

### System information

Arch Linux, Niri

### Issue description

Some shortcuts bindings, seems to be only ones which are not direct keys and are transformed by the keyboard layout, cannot be triggered.

Notably `%` and `@` can still be typed within text fields, so the issue seems isolated to shortcuts.

### Steps to reproduce

- Bind `Toggle Access as Unique Name` to <kbd>Percent</kbd>, <kbd>Percent (Physical)</kbd> (expected not to work) or <kbd>Percent (Unicode)</kbd>
- Shortcut doesn't trigger when <kbd>Shift</kbd>+<kbd>5</kbd> is pressed on a US layout keyboard

Additionally:
- In the 3D editor viewport, the default binding for `Toggle Grid` bound to <kbd>NumberSign</kbd> cannot be triggered

Reproducible in both Wayland and X11 (through Xwayland with [xwayland-satellite](https://github.com/Supreeeme/xwayland-satellite))

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

summary: Adding "-loop" suffix to imported animation name will not enable loop mode

description.text:
### Tested versions

Linux - 4.3 stable and 4.4.1 stable



### System information

Linux - 4.3 stable and 4.4.1 stable

### Issue description

So I create an animation in blender and add the "-loop" suffix to it's name. 

In Godot, if I open the imported glb file as a scene, the animation will be set to loop in the scene's animation player.

But if i double click the glb file to open the import settings, the loop mode for the animation is not set there. And if I set the save path for the animations and do a reimport, the "saved" animation files will not be set to loop.
(And any information about whether the animations should loop or not will be lost at that point since the loop mode wasn't set and the suffixes were removed before doing the reimport.)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Import an animation with the "-loop" suffix added to it's name (Or open the example project..)
2. Open the imported glb file as a scene. See that the animation loops in the animation player (as it should)
3. Open the import settings for the glb file. See that the loop mode is not set.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[animloopbugproject.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21342562/animloopbugproject.zip)

summary: Expose set_inline_object_handlers to GDScript

description.text: The functionality for inline objects was added with #105724, however it wasn't exposed to GDScript. This does so and adds the related documentation. I wrote a small script (the one in the documentation) to test the functionality by simulating inlay hints for types: ```gdscript extends CodeEdit func _init() -> void: set_inline_object_handlers(_parser, _drawer, _click_handler) func _parser(t: String) -> Array[Dictionary]: var r: Array[Dictionary] = [] var font := get_theme_font("font") var font_height := font.get_height(get_theme_font_size("font_size")) var re = RegEx.create_from_string("var\\s+(\\w+)\\s*:?\\s*=") var matches := re.search_all(t) for m in matches: var hint_text := ": the type of %s" % m.get_string(1) var requested_width := font.get_string_size(hint_text).x r.append({ "column": m.get_end(1), "variable_name": m.get_string(1), "width_ratio": (requested_width + 6) / font_height, "text": hint_text }) return r func _drawer(info: Dictionary, col_rect: Rect2): var font := get_theme_font("font") var font_size := get_theme_font_size("font_size") var string_size = Vector2(font.get_string_size(info.text).x, font.get_ascent(font_size)) draw_rect(Rect2(col_rect.position + Vector2(0, 0), string_size).grow_individual(0, 3, 0, font.get_descent(font_size) + 3), Color.BLACK) draw_string(font, col_rect.position + string_size.y * Vector2.DOWN + Vector2(0, 0), info.text, HORIZONTAL_ALIGNMENT_LEFT, -1, font_size, Color(1, 1, 1, 0.5)) func _click_handler(info: Dictionary, _col_rect: Rect2): print("Hint of variable %s was clicked" % info.variable_name) ``` and this is what it looks like, as expected: Closes godotengine/godot-proposals#12822

summary: Scene (glb/gltf) Import Root Type can't get class unless direct descendant of Base Node

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.x (all 4.x.x versions tested)


### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable.mono - macOS Sequoia (15.4.0) - Multi-window, 3 monitors - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M1 Pro (Apple7) - Apple M1 Pro (10 threads)

### Issue description

Current Setup Used for MRP: Godot v4.4.1.stable.mono - macOS Sequoia (15.4.0) - Multi-window, 3 monitors - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M1 Pro (Apple7) - Apple M1 Pro (10 threads)

When importing a scene and setting Root Type if the type is not a direct descendant of a built-in type, the following error is thrown.

```ERROR: Cannot get class 'BaseType'.```

In the example MRP

`class_name BaseType extends Node3D`

`class_name ChildType extends Node3D`

Root Type = BaseType ✅  
Root Type = ChildType ❌ 

### Steps to reproduce

Try to import a scene setting the Root Type to `ChildType`

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21337772/mrp.zip)

summary: Android export broken after upgrading from Godot 4.4.1 to 4.5 – missing .pck and broken 3D models after reimport

description.text: ### Tested versions Upgraded from 4.4.1 to 4.5 beta 3 ### System information macos ### Issue description After upgrading a project from Godot 4.4.1 to 4.5 and exporting to Android, the build fails with the following error: Error Couldn't load project data at path "." is the .pck file missing? If you've renamed the executable, the associated .pck file should also be renamed to match the executable name (without the extension). To debug the issue, I created a new Godot 4.5 project and manually re-added assets from the original one. The new clean build was able to export successfully at first. However, after reimpor ting the 3D model assets (GLTF/OBJ/etc.), Godot started reimporting them automatically. Once the reimport process completed, the Android export succeeded — but all 3D models were broken visually (e.g., invisible, distorted, or materials lost). This issue did not happen in 4.4.1. ### Steps to reproduce Steps to reproduce: Open a working 4.4.1 project in Godot 4.5. Try to export to Android → fails with missing .pck error. Create a new project in 4.5, move project files over manually. After reimporting 3D models, export succeeds. Open the game on Android → all 3D models are visually broken. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) n/a

summary: [macOS] Use "file" icon for bundles in the file dialogs.

description.text: Built-in file dialogs can detect [bundles](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bundle_(macOS)) and allow selecting them as files, but were using folder icon for them, which might be confusing. This PR changes it to use generic file icon (but with folder color) instead. `AppIcon.icon` is bundle this screenshot:

summary: C# Manually created plugin isn't detected

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: Godot v4.4.1.mono, Godot v4.5.beta3.mono

### System information

Windows 11, Rider 2025.1

### Issue description

Plugin isn't detected when manually created files for plugin (`plugin.cfg` + plugin script).
The warning is visible in Output when entering Project settings: 
```
WARNING: editor/plugins/editor_plugin_settings.cpp:96 - Plugin config at "res://addons/MyPlugin/plugin.cfg" is missing the following keys: "plugin/name","plugin/author","plugin/version","plugin/description","plugin/script"
```

If plugin is created through editor, it works without issue.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create new project
2. Create folder `addons`
3. Create plugin folder, i.e. `TestPlugin`
4. Create `plugin.cfg` with content
```
[plugin]

name = "TestPlugin"
description = ""
author = "Test"
version = "1.0"
script = "TestPlugin.cs"
```
5. Create `TestPlugin.cs` which extends `EditorPlugin` and uses attribute `Tool`
6. In Godot, open Project>Project Settings
7. Observe warning in Output. Plugin isn't visible on Plugins list

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Please note how GDScript Plugin is working, but C# Plugin isn't working.
[Test1.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21335799/Test1.zip)

summary: Not showing all Godot Versions in MacOS Launchpad

description.text: ### Tested versions This seems to happen with any Godot version. I tested Godot 3.6.1 Stable Mono, Godot 4.4.1 Stable Mono, and Godot 4.5 beta 3 Mono ### System information MacOS 15.5 ### Issue description When having multiple Godot versions on macOS, macOS’s Launchpad only shows the latest version of Godot. This might be because each Godot version has the same **CFBundleIdentifier** (found in the app's Info.plist file) making MacOS only show the latest Godot version. I tried to change the **CFBundleIdentifier** myself but it didn't fix anything so it might be something else. ### Steps to reproduce - Have Multiple Godot Versions in the Application folder (I additionally put them into a file called Godots. e.g. /Applications/Godots/Godot_4.4.app) (I also tested them just being in the Application folder) - Open Launchpad and find the one Godot app ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) N/A

summary: Clip children clips CollisionShape2D visualization.

description.text: ### Tested versions 4.4.1 ### System information Windows 11 - Nvidia RTX 4070 - intel i5 13600kf ### Issue description As you can see in the screenshots with the clip enabled (clip only as well as clip + draw) or disabled. the depiction of collision shapes is also clipped. I'd say this shouldn't happen since collision shapes are not part of standard visuals but rather serve to let the user know the bounds for the collisions (the collision itself is not clipped, only the collision shape). You can imagine how this makes working with colliders difficult under these conditions :). Disabled Clip Only Clip + Draw ### Steps to reproduce 1. Add ColorRect. 2. Give the ColorRect a CollisionShape2D as a child. 3. Set CircleShape2D as the Shape of the CollisionShape2D. 4. On the ColorRect go to Visibility section in the Inspector and change Clip Children to Clip Only. 5. Obsserve the debug shape is now limited only to the ColorRect area. (This is not ColorRect issue, I used it just as an example, other Control/Node2D nodes which have the Clip Children setting behave the same.) ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) N/A. If it's really needed I'll add a simple one but I can't do it right now, I'll be doing it later but it's simple to test if nothing else.

summary: Editor randomly crashes when select a tree node and close the scene tab

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Not reproducible in earlier versions

MacOS - godot 4.5 master-71a9948157

### Issue description

After applying PR #108122, when closing the scene tab, the pointers in LocalVector<Node *> node_previous_selection become dangling pointers, which causes random crash.

Reference: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/108122#issuecomment-3092591332

### Steps to reproduce

- select a tree node
- close the scene tab
- editor crashes

summary: Enabling Fog in WorldEnvironment changes background color even when Sky Affect is set to 0

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in: 4.4.1.stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - AMD Radeon RX 7800 XT - AMD Ryzen 7 7700 8-Core Processor (16 threads)

### Issue description

When having the Background Mode in WorldEnvironment set to Clear Color or Custom Color, enabling Fog at all (doesn't matter whether Exponential or Depth Fog) affects the color of the background. Even when the fog "density" is set to 0, effectively making the fog completely transparent and having "Sky Affect" set to 0, toggling between having the fog on and off very clearly shows a significant color difference (Having the Fog enabled makes the background color darker and more saturated)

### Steps to reproduce

- Create an empty 3D scene
- Add a WorldEnvironment
- Change the Background Mode to Custom Color and set some color for the background
- Enable the Fog and in the Fog settings set Sky Affect to 0.0
- You can now toggle the Fog on and off to see that it clearly affects the color of the background

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

summary: Screen Readers Skip Options When Navigating Some Menus

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.5-beta2 and 4.5-beta3

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta3 - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA RTX A1000 Laptop GPU - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-12800H (20 threads) - 31.67 GiB memory

### Issue description

When using a screen reader to navigate the menu bar containing options like new scene, new inherited scene, open scene, etc., some options get skipped, e.g.:
- Go into menu, hear: "New Scene   1 of 23"
- Hit down arrow, hear: "New Inherited Scene...   2 of 23"
- Hit down arrow, hear: "Open Scene...   3 of 23"
- Hit down arrow, hear: "Open Recent   5 of 23"

This is present in all tabs, not just the one dealing with scenes/scripts.

In addition, sometimes pressing down or up arrow skips to a seemingly random option within the menus. This is often after one switches menus, e.g., from the menu containing options for things like project settings back to one containing the new scene option. Pressing down arrow at this point often snaps one to 8th, 12th, 17th option with no rhyme or reason behind it. This behavior is inconsistent.

Incidentally, this bug (skipping menu options) also impacts usage of the inspector. It's particularly impactful when selecting things like value types for a dictionary (some options just downright get skipped when using up/down arrow keys).


Expected behavior: one should be able to arrow up/down inside the menus and not have the sporadic option skips.

### Steps to reproduce

- Create any project inside Godot.
- Go to the menu bar containing the options for things like creating new scene
- Use up/down arrows with screen reader
- Witness the bug.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

summary: Autocompletion omits parentheses when calling a function in a lambda that's passed as an argument

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.5.beta3.official [4d1f26e1f]

### System information

unrelated

### Issue description

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c10ede82-d36d-485d-ba05-bbd0969ab2cd

When autocompleting method names in say `signal.connect()`, parentheses don't get automatically inserted since you want to pass the method, not call it.

However, when building a lambda function in the place it's passed as an argument, calling a function inside of this one will not get its parentheses added, which is tedious.



### Steps to reproduce

```gdscript
func f() -> void:
    ready.connect(print_orphan_nodes) # convenient
    ready.connect(
        func(): # less convenient
            print_orphan_nodes()
    )

```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

any

summary: In embeded game, when it may be pimpossible to pick some 2D/3D nodes

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.4.1
4.5.beta2
v4.5.beta3.official [4d1f26e1f]

### System information

Windows 11 - OpenGL API 3.3.0 NVIDIA 576.88 - Compatibility - Using Device: NVIDIA - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 Laptop GPU

### Issue description

When a scene is crouded with a lot of nodes, is ofter useful to use the ALT+Right click tool to pick nodes by name.
Also when nodes moves and dies fast is useful to pause the game to take all the time you want to pick it.
But when the amount of nodes in your scene is so large that the popup menu that appears needs an scrollbar, the scrollabr does not work (presumibli) because the game is paused

### Steps to reproduce

- In a blank scene create a ColorRect covering the entire screen
- start duplicating in dozen of times ( my computer fits 38 notes in the list, but others monitors could fit more or less)
- Start the game with embeded mode enabled
- Pause the game
- change input mode from **Input** to **2D**
- Alt+RightClick anywhere, a long popup menu with all nodes in mouse's position will appear, if the list of nodes is long enough it will have a scrollbar
- Try to make the scrollbar work with the mouse wheel, it will not work

I discovered once an item is hovered you can use arrow keys to move down and show more items bellow


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[test-bug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21323980/test-bug.zip)

summary: Items in a PopupMenu cannot be selected even when checkable

description.text:
### Tested versions

Tested in 4.4 stable, 4.5 beta 3

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Mobile) - integrated Intel(R) Arc(TM) Graphics (Intel Corporation; 32.0.101.6874) - Intel(R) Core(TM) Ultra 5 125H (18 threads)

### Issue description

Items added to a PopupMenu (either by GUI dock or via code) cannot be selected, even when the checkable property is set. The items can still be hovered over with the mouse or focused with the keyboard, but clicking on them has no effect on their checked property. (If hide_on_checkable_item_selection is true, the menu will hide when an item is clicked, but calling show() reveals nothing was selected.) This has been tested with both the radiobutton and checkbutton options.
Interestingly, the "id_pressed" and "index_pressed" signals still activate when the item is clicked, but they do not seem to activate when an associated accelerator key is pressed. 
As the documentation makes no mention of setting up your own button behavior (and a radiobutton option seems to imply that it should automatically deselect others) I assume that these actions are supposed to be reflected on the menu items.

### Steps to reproduce

Create a scene with a PopupMenu as a child. Set hide_on_checkable_item_selection to false. Add item(s) with code (e.g. add_check_item()) or the GUI. Connect a timer to the show() method and run the scene. You will see that hovering with the mouse / focusing with the keyboard works, but has no effect on the buttons. Alternatively, see the MRP for a more detailed implementation that includes items from code, from the GUI, and signal behavior.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21323848/mrp.zip) (Godot 4.4)

summary: Not all TTS voices on Windows 11 are picked up by Godot

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.4.1 Stable, 4.3 Stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-10700K CPU @ 3.80GHz (16 threads)

### Issue description

When using `DisplayServer.tts_get_voices()` or `DisplayServer.tts_get_voices_for_language()` on Windows 11 it searches specifically in the "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Speech\Voices\Tokens" registry folder, but not all voices on the system are stored there. More languages and voices are in this folder: "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Speech_OneCore\Voices\Tokens"

For instance, the French voice "Hortense" is stored in "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Speech\Voices\Tokens\TTS_MS_FR-FR_HORTENSE_11.0" and Godot is able to detect it, but cannot detect the Greek voice "Stefanos" which is only stored in "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Speech_OneCore\Voices\Tokens\MSTTS_V110_elGR_Stefanos"

I was able to copy the registry key information for the voice "Stefanos" from the "Speech_OneCore" information into the "Speech" folder and Godot was able to detect it and use it, but this is not an ideal workaround:
- Requires users to edit their registry
- Windows update has wiped this copied information on more than one occasion, going back to square one

### Steps to reproduce

1. Go to Time & Language > Speech on Windows 11.
2. Manage Voices > Add voices
3. Download Greek language pack
4. _Optional: Download an additional language like French_ to test against.
5. Use DisplayServer.tts_get_voices() in a print command in Godot to show the Greek voice is not found, but the EN (and FR, if downloaded) voice(s) are available.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

summary: HDR 2D debanding is broken with Mobile rendering method

description.text: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in 2be2cbb720e4b2c2fa11e677c73d4371c4ab47b9 onwards - Not reproducible in Godot 4.4.1 ### System information Any ### Issue description When I wrote https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/107782, I forgot that HDR 2D still uses a 10-bit viewport buffer on the Mobile Rendering method, so this means that viewport-based debanding must still be active with HDR 2D enabled, but it should be 10-bit aligned dithering rather than 8-bit aligned dithering. End result is that non-material based effects like Glow effects no longer have functioning debanding: | 4.4.1 | masater | --- | --- | | ### Steps to reproduce - Create a project with Mobile rendering method - Enable HDR 2D - Set up a scene that uses environment effects like Glow - Enable debanding - Note that it doesn't work on the environment effects ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [mobile-debanding.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21322782/mobile-debanding.zip)

summary: 3D Selection Behaviour - Box select is selecting objects behind other objects

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducable in: v4.4.1.stable.arch_linux

### System information

OS: Arch 

### Issue description

In my opinion the 3d selection behavior is really unusable. The main issue is with box selection in editor. When using box selection it selects the everything within the box (even objects not in view that are behind what im trying to select).

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6081a6cd-c9f3-4669-9da5-a664e0400c47

Not sure if this is also related but clicking the mesh of an object is not selecting the correct object it selects the object behind it. 

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/62cc6fab-a803-4ab9-928b-4c1131573ec0

I did see that there were some other issues reported already regarding this but they were all closed with no reference to the current open one. In my opinion this is a very high priority feature. I'd love to take a look at fixing it myself if I can. 

EDIT:

Another issue i found as well. When selecting objects with a single click you must select basically right next to the origin of the object. Clicking on the mesh itself does nothing.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/28645238-1321-48b7-a87b-c2533f9fa070


### Steps to reproduce

Add a few cubes to the scene. Add some cubes behind the other cubes. Rotate your view and try to select the cubes in front. 

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21322272/mrp.zip)

Prevents user from setting zero thread counts via GUI, and the worker thread pool will auto detect thread count if it is set to zero.

Fixes #107733

summary: LightmapGI: Environment sky intensity is not taken into account for ambient light baking when physical light units are enabled

description.text: - *Related to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/74139, https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/74899 and https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/104174.* ### Tested versions - Reproducible in: 4.5.beta https://github.com/godotengine/godot/commit/71a9948157dc2d5cc71fcb456c9d96656678757d ### System information Godot v4.5.beta (71a994815) - Fedora Linux 42 (KDE Plasma Desktop Edition) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 (nvidia; 575.64.03) - AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 62.33 GiB memory ### Issue description Environment sky intensity is not taken into account for ambient light baking when physical light units are enabled. Only the non-physical sky energy parameter is taken into account instead. **Edit:** After some testing, it appears to work but is inconsistent. Further testing is ### Energy Multiplier = 1.0, Intensity = 300 nt ### Energy Multiplier = 1.0, Intensity = 30,000 nt *Lighting appears different due to brighter IBL, but the lightmap is too bright in both cases.* ### Energy Multiplier = 0.01, Intensity = 300 nt *Ambient light is more suitable now, but it's too bright compared to the sky.* ### Energy Multiplier = 0.01, Intensity = 30,000 nt *Also notice how the brightness of dynamic object lighting on the sphere of the left is now brighter for some reason.* ### Steps to reproduce - Configure the Environment resource in the WorldEnvironment node to adjust sky light energy and intensity. Try different values that *should* compensate for each other, then bake lightmaps in the MRP and compare results on static and dynamic objects. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [Lightmap_dark.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21318681/Lightmap_dark.zip)

summary: [Web] Fix Web MouseWheel scrolling

summary: [4.5-beta3] Key release event is missed when embedded game window loses focus on macOS

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: v4.5.beta3.official [4d1f26e1f] up to v4.5.dev4.official [209a446e3]
- Reproducible only on `macOS`

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta3 - macOS Sequoia (15.5.0) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M1 Pro (Apple7) - Apple M1 Pro (10 threads) - 16.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

When running a game in the editor's embedded window on macOS, if a key is held down and the window loses focus (e.g., by clicking on the editor UI), the subsequent key-release event is not registered by the game window.

When focus is returned to the game window, Godot will not register the key as released until it is pressed and released again while the window is focused.

This behavior not observed when not using embeded game window. Release events are fired even without focus for seperate game windows.


### Steps to reproduce

- Open the MRP
- Make sure "Embed game on next play" is **on** (floating or not)
- Test `WASD` keys, press and hold one or more
- Click something on editor window outside the embedded game window (eg. hierachy)
- Release held keys
- UI still indicates that the keys are still pressed (released event missed)

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[mrp-input-stuck.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21315123/mrp-input-stuck.zip)

summary: Inherit in Auto Translate Mode is always generated by POT generation.

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Mobile) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1060 6GB (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-9400F CPU @ 2.90GHz (6 threads)

### Issue description

If the auto_translate_mode of a node is set to AutoTranslateMode.AUTO_TRANSLATE_MODE_INHERIT, it is always generated by POT generation, regardless of the parent node.
If internationalization/rendering/root_node_auto_translate in the project settings is set to false and the root node is INHERIT, it will also be generated.

### Steps to reproduce

Create a scene for the Control node.
Set the Control node's auto_translate_mode to AutoTranslateMode.AUTO_TRANSLATE_MODE_DISABLED.
Add a Label node to the child nodes.
Set the text of the Label node to “Test”.
Set the Label node's auto_translate_mode to AutoTranslateMode.AUTO_TRANSLATE_MODE_INHERIT.
Add the created scene to the POT generation.
Generate the POT.
The POT contains “Test”.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[pot_mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21326746/pot_mrp.zip)

summary: Fix jump when cutting a selection in Gridmap

description.text:
solves https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/101428

Please note that I chose to create a variable `cursor_gridpos` so that I have easy access to where is the cursor when the user press X to cut the selection. Hence, I also replaced the occurences of `Vectori(cell[0], cell[1], cell[2])` with `cursor_gridpos`.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5fc971ea-291f-46b7-baab-93247a24a496

Please note that I ignored the Y axis for now, I will complete the PR to work with Y if we are all happy with the current changes.

I tested on:
- the 4 quadrants XZ
- selections that starts from the 4 different corners  (e.g. start selection from bottom-left to top-left, etc)

summary: Allow concurrent unbinding and binding of signal arguments in editor

description.text:
Removed exclusivity between unbind and bind in many places, both on display elements, connection dock and within serialization.

A signal can now be connected with both unbound and bound arguments through the editor, previously setting unbinds above 0 would grey out the binds section. 

This also works with the append_source parameter.

*Bugsquad edit:* Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/12801 Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/7542

summary: Fix scene tree node folding

description.text:
A small edit that fixes expanding a node folding in scene tree editor.
The problem was calling `set_display_folded` and emitting `editor_state_changed` was collapsing the node but not expanding it.

summary: [Android] Add export option to use "scrcpy" to run project from editor.

description.text:
Adds export option to use [scrcpy](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy) to run the project on a virtual screen and stream it back to the device running editor.

- Can be used to view and control device screen directly from the editor device.
- Can be used to run multiple apps on the same device.
- Virtual screen can be used to test different screen sizes / DPIs.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/49cb302a-ae33-49fa-84c8-b30513d4879b

summary: Shader built-in `VIEWPORT_SIZE` is affected by `scaling_3d_scale`
Transform gizmo arrows are inconsistent

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.5.beta2

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta2 - macOS Sequoia (15.3.0) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M2 (Apple8) - Apple M2 (8 threads) - 8.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

Lately the editors transform gizmo has felt off to me, and I was able to track down two problems. Call them problem A and problem B. Both problems have something to do with the perspective camera, and are only reproducible in perspective mode.

Problem A: The arrows have a tendency to move more in one direction depending on perspective. Look closely at the movement of my mouse vs the result. Hopefully you can see that the result is very different as the object approaches the camera.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f1ebaaad-d3eb-4e55-bfbf-b6adf9015b07

Problem B: The dragging direction doesn't always follow the arrow. This happens most when the axis appears distorted due to perspective.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b3784380-e887-4550-991a-b2436afae744

### Steps to reproduce

Reproduce problem A
1. Select an object
2. Position the camera so that the arrow points toward you
3. Drag the arrow. It will appear to slow down as it approaches the camera
4. Orbit 180 degrees to the other side, so that the arrow points away from you
5. Drag the arrow. It will appear to speed up as it approaches the camera

Reproduce problem B
1. Select an object
2. Position the camera so that the arrow points toward you
3. By turning the camera, position the arrow in the corner of the screen to maximise perspective distortion
4. Drag the arrow and observe that dragging perpendicular to the axis has an unwanted effect

Increasing the FOV of the editor camera may also help to reproduce the issue

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

-

summary: Infinite recursion while trying to use recursively referencing nodes as keys for a `Dictionary`

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: v4.4.1.stable.official, v4.5.beta3.
- Not reproducible in: prior v4.4, as typed dictionaries were not a thing, thus there was unresonably hard to use nodes as a keys.

### System information

Fedora Linux 42 (KDE Plasma Desktop Edition) on Wayland - Wayland display driver, Single-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 Ti (nvidia; 575.64) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-9600KF CPU @ 3.70GHz (6 threads)

### Issue description

If you use `Node` as a key in a dictionary, sometimes the Godot Editor will go into infinite recursion, printing:
```
...
ERROR: Max recursion reached
at: write (core/variant/variant_parser.cpp:2116)
ERROR: Max recursion reached
at: write (core/variant/variant_parser.cpp:2116)
ERROR: Max recursion reached
at: write (core/variant/variant_parser.cpp:2116)
...
```
Completely not responding. The only thing remains is to kill the proccess.
Imagine a setup of three nodes:
- Node1 exported references to Node2 and Node3
- Node2 exported references to Node1 and Node3
- Node3 exported references to Node1 and Node2
When i add a single node into a dictionary, everything's fine, but the moment i drag a second node (exact order does not matter, i can even try and add the same node a second time) into a 'New Key' field, editor hangs.

It also happens in the following setup of two nodes, which i think is really strange:
- Node1 exported references to Node2 and Node2 (as two separate vars)
- Node2 exported references to Node1 (as either of them)

It **does not** hang in a simpler setup of two nodes, but the editor still complains about maximum recursion being reached in the output:
- Node1 exported reference to Node2
- Node2 exported reference to Node1

> Note that every test is done where all the nodes (except the one with an exported dictionary) have the same script.

The interesing part is that after manually editing the `.tscn` file in a text editor, it works and shows fine in the editor. It looks to me like some hashing issue in the dictionary export UI. 

I already refactored my code not to use this stupid scheme, but it still might be useful is some cases, especially as typed dictionaries added an ability to use `Node`s as keys.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Download a project.
2. Select a `Broken` node.
3. Put one of `Node1`, `Node2` or `Node3` as key and whatever value you want, then press 'Add Key/Value Pair' button.
4. Put one of `Node1`, `Node2` or `Node3` as key and see the editor hang.


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[dicts_with_nodes_as_keys.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21306616/dicts_with_nodes_as_keys.zip)

summary: D3D12 shader compilation fails for Fog shader

description.text:
### Tested versions

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/commit/db1b6b93562ff93adb2808b2c93ab3c63074b301

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta (f92f1ce9c) - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated Intel(R) Arc(TM) A770 Graphics (Intel Corporation; 32.0.101.6913) - AMD Ryzen 5 7600X 6-Core Processor (12 threads) - 55.60 GiB memory

### Issue description

PR https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/108028 introduced the following pragma to the fog shader.
```glsl
#pragma use_vulkan_memory_model
```
This causes the SPIR-V -> NIR -> DXIL converter to fail with the following errors.
```
SPIR-V WARNING:
    In file godot-mesa\src\compiler\spirv\spirv_to_nir.c:4553
    Unsupported SPIR-V capability: SpvCapabilityVulkanMemoryModel (5345)
    28 bytes into the SPIR-V binary
SPIR-V parsing FAILED:
    In file godot-mesa\src\compiler\spirv\spirv_to_nir.c:4735
    Vulkan memory model is unsupported by this driver
    60 bytes into the SPIR-V binary
```
As it stands, this causes D3D12 to fail on Forward+

### Steps to reproduce

Simply open any project on Forward+

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[d-3d-12.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21302973/d-3d-12.zip)

summary: Screen-space AA doesn't work properly with 3D scaling

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.4 stable and 4.5 beta3

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta3 - Arch Linux #1 ZEN SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu, 10 Jul 2025 17:10:03 +0000 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated Intel(R) Iris(R) Xe Graphics (RPL-P) - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-13900H (20 threads) - 31.04 GiB memory

### Issue description

When using 3D scaling, the screen-space anti-aliasing (FXAA or SMAA) is applied after the image is scaled up, which means it doesn't get rid of aliasing. The solution would be to apply the anti-aliasing filter before the image is scaled up.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Enable FXAA or SMAA
2. Set the 3D scaling to 0.25
3. (optional) Set the mipmap bias to 2 so that the texture filtering doesn't look terrible and crusty

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[3d_scaling_with_screen_space_aa.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21302861/3d_scaling_with_screen_space_aa.zip)

summary: [SplitContainer] Can't set touch dragger icon theme override

- *Production edit: Follow-up to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/105994.*

### Issue description

Changing the theme icon for the new touch dragger has no effect.

This issue is fixed by PR #108718.

### Steps to reproduce

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

See PR #108718

summary: peer_disconnect() with the forced variable set to true asymmetrically disconnects.

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.3.stable, 4.4.1.stable, and 4.5.beta3

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta3 - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - AMD Ryzen 5 7600X 6-Core Processor (12 threads) - 31.10 GiB memory

### Issue description

Using `MultiplayerPeer.disconnect_peer` to remove peers with the "forced" parameter set to true seems to break disconnecting. 

The client peer will disconnect and remove host from list of peers (using `get_peers()`) while the server will keep the peer id in their list. This already is an issue, but if the client attempts to rejoin, connection fails and an error is printed as follows: 

`E 0:00:26:264   put_packet: Invalid target peer: 1713975333
  <C++ Error>   Condition "target_peer != 0 && !peers.has(Math::abs(target_peer))" is true. Returning: ERR_INVALID_PARAMETER
  <C++ Source>  modules/enet/enet_multiplayer_peer.cpp:337 @ put_packet()`

This is different to the behavior when "forced" is set to false. The documentation states the intended behavior should just be that no `peer_disconnected` signal is triggered.

### Steps to reproduce

MRP created and attached. In the MRP, run the program and:

- Press "1" to host on the first instance.
- Switch to the second instance, and press "2" to join.
- Switch to hosting instance, and press "3" to kick.
- Switch back to second instance and attempt rejoining.

At any point, press "Enter" or "Space" to print the list of connected peers (using `get_peers()`) from either end.

By default, the "forced attribute is set to false to show intended behavior with no issues. Toggle the "forced" parameter by pressing "4" or before running in editor, and repeat the listed steps.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)


[mrp-force-disconnect.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21301910/mrp-force-disconnect.zip)

summary: [Jolt] Sleeping RigidBody3D Stack Woken by Area3D

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.5-beta3 with Jolt

### System information

Windows 11 Forward+ nvidia 3070 Jolt Physics

### Issue description

## Summary  
When a stack of RigidBody3D nodes enters sleep mode, sweeping another body, one configured with no collision layers or masks, through the stack unexpectedly wakes the entire stack, despite no collision layers in common.

## Expected Behavior  
A body with no collision layers and no collision mask (and with contact monitoring off) should not interact with or wake other physics bodies. The sleeping stack should remain asleep when such a “ghost” body passes through it.

## Actual Behavior  
Despite having zero layers/masks, the swept body still triggers a wake-up on the entire stack of RigidBody3D nodes, causing them to wobble and re-simulate physics.

## Additional Information  
- Possible cause: broadphase overlap tests or Jolt’s internal wake-up logic do not check collision layers/masks before waking sleeping bodies.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a scene with 10 identical 1×1×1 boxes:  
   - Each box is a RigidBody3D
   - Attach a BoxCollider3D to each.  
   - Position them in a perfect vertical stack (Z-axis separation = box height).  
2. Start the scene and wait for the boxes to settle; they should wobble briefly then enter sleep mode.  
3. Instance or spawn a new 1×1×1 box:  
   - RigidBody3D set to “Sleeping” on start.  
   - Collision → Layer: None, Mask: None.  
   - Contact monitoring disabled.  
   - Directly sweep/move this frozen box through the side of the sleeping stack using a script
4. Observe the stack: instead of remaining at rest, all 10 boxes wake and begin wobbling again.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

It's easier/faster to create this in Godot than downloading a MRP.
